MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marie Quashnock for (petitioners) Andrew Swerdlow & Gianmatteo Costanza. Emilie Calhoun for (administrator) Paul Fredinberg.

Ms. Calhoun stated that they filed a request for a continuance. Ms. Quashnock stated that she wants this to be heard, that the requested continuance is lengthy, that there is a lot going on with the property and that the bank agrees with the petition. Ms. Calhoun stated that they will file a demurrer to the petition, that the parties have no standing, that they objected to the ex parte petition and that she does not know when the petition will be heard. Ms. Quashnock stated that construction is complete, that they need to transition the loan and that the continuance needs to be short. Ms. Calhoun stated that they cannot do anything until business counsel is in place. Ms. Quashnock stated that there are no business entity issues here, that the petition is covered in the statute and that it is just a petition to determine title. The Court stated that it is inclined to give time to find counsel. Ms. Calhoun stated that if the continuance is short they will need permission to obtain counsel. The petition to determine title to real property and require transfer of real property to limited liability company was continued to April 18, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the instruction to have business counsel on board.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Feb-01-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alex Weingarten for Leslie Eggers. Virginia Peiser for Tricia Curreri. Emilie Calhoun for (administrator) Paul Fredinburg.

Mr. Weingarten reported that the parties had been ordered to mediation, that there was one discreet issue to resolve, that there has been no formal discovery, that all information obtained has been done informally and that they are trying to minimize costs. Ms. Calhoun stated that she does not have authority to mediate, that it is a further delay tactic, that there is lack of and counter cooperation, that the petition is inconsistent, that the separate and community property listed is identical, that they need 3 months to conduct discovery and that mediation is not the proper course. Ms. Peiser stated that she has no authority to mediate, that they would like to address that there was no response until 8/12 to the petition, cited cases regarding surviving spouses and read passages to the court and stated that Ms. Eggers waived her rights to the decedents property. Mr. Weingarten stated that they are not trying to delay, that counsel knows how to ask for things but not how to follow up, there has been no discovery requests to Google and that discovery was propounded to them and they responded. The Court stated that it is inclined to deny the spousal property petition or to take it off calendar and asked what effect it will have. Mr. Weingarten stated that it is fine and that they can always refile. Ms. Calhoun stated that because of the status of information exchange Google has produced documents, that what they do not have are emails, that they hoped Ms. Eggers would provide them so that they will not have to subpoena Google, that there is a lack of cooperation, that they would like the spousal property petition denied without prejudice and that a dismissal will leave a shade of uncertainty to the appraiser. Ms. Peiser stated that as a matter of sensible use of judicial resources, that the agreement Ms. Eggers signed is binding, that she waived her claim to spousal rights and that they prefer that the petition be denied. Mr. Weingarten stated that they do not think it is inefficient for the administrator to appraise all assets of the estate, that the ultimate resolution should be done through the main petition, referenced the cases Ms. Peiser cited and stated that there has been no final order in this case and that the do not need this petition to go forward. The Court denied the petition for spousal property without prejudice and urged the parties to informal mediation regarding if the appraisal of the entire assets makes sense.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. Court Reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR #13293, 415-274-9977 (Reported)

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Nov-02-2015 Continued To Feb-01-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alex Weingarten for (petitioner) Leslie Eggers. Virginia Peiser for (claimant) Tricia Curreri. Emilie Calhoun for (administrator) Paul Fredinburg.

Ms. Calhoun stated that there was a late filed reply brief that the court may have not been able to review and that she is prepared to address it. The Court asked if everyone was in agreement to proceed. Ms. Calhoun stated that she would not proceed if the court agrees with the objections, discussed section 7a & 7b of the petition along with 13651(a)(2) & (a)(4), that there is no description of the separate property and that it is not clear what is being alleged. Ms. Peiser stated that it is a confusing argument as to who is the surviving spouse, that they were only married 13 months and then separated and that the petitioner needs to demonstrate accretions to the decedents accounts. Mr. Weingarten stated that they need to proceed with discovery to be able to describe the property, that the marriage lasted longer than 13 months, that they never got divorced and that the community property ceased to accrue at the time they separated. The Court asked for a time frame on discovery. Mr. Weingarten stated that a time estimate was premature, that they are not sure of the cooperation they will get and that they need a forensic accounting. Ms. Calhoun spoke of the opposition brief, cited a case as to why the spousal property petition should not be granted, that her client is exposed to a liability, that Ms. Eggers gets the advantage to the creditors procedure and asks that the court not rule today. Ms. Peiser stated that they oppose the brief and cited the Estate of Daniel and the Estate of Leahy regarding marital dissolution. Mr. Weingarten stated that discovery is appropriate and that they will expedite the process. The Court stated that it is not prepared to resolve the matter today and that it will either continue for a period of time for discovery or take the matter off calendar. Mr. Weingarten stated that the amount of time depends on the cooperation and that he would like the matter continued. Ms. Calhoun stated that they have been set back 6 months on discovery, that the track record does not indicate that this will go any faster and requested that the order on the "Google document" be modified to say without prejudice. Mr. Weingarten stated that the order is perfectly clear and that they need to follow appropriate procedures. The petition for spousal property was continued to February 1, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and that if the matter is not resolved at that time it will be taken off calendar.

Ms. Calhoun reported that the competing petition has been dismissed, that the opposing party requests that bond be posted, that they are willing to do so and are ready to file bond once appointed, that the estranged wife wants to give her opinion as to how distribution is to be made, that the father (Mr. Fredinburg) has priority and that Ms. Eggers and the decedent were going through a divorce proceeding. Mr. Lorenzen stated that they do not disagree with the points made by Ms. Calhoun but want to be sure bond is posted. Ms. Peiser stated that Ms. Curreri is a named beneficiary of an account and a 401k, that they support the petition of Mr. Fredinburg, that they do not feel bond is necessary and that they have no issue with his competency. The court stated that bond will be in the amount of $761,000.00. Ms. Calhoun stated that they are requesting full authority, that a Spousal Property Petition was filed and set for August 18, 2015 and requests that the petition be moved to a date that Judge Cheng will be able to hear it. The court continued the Spousal Property Petition to August 24, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition for Letters of Administration (petitioner Leslie Eggers) is denied. The Petition for Letters of Administration (petitioner Paul Fredinberg) is granted (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Park. (Not Reported)
